Starting a Broker • 2/8/2026 • 7 min

Payment Systems for Forex Brokers: Solutions Overview

How to connect and configure payment solutions for a forex broker: bank cards, e-wallets, and cryptocurrency payments.

MakeTrades Team
Эксперт MakeTrades

Traditional Payment Methods

Bank card processing remains the primary deposit method for most retail traders. Securing a merchant account for forex is challenging due to the high-risk classification. Work with specialized payment service providers (PSPs) who understand the industry. Expect processing fees of 2.5-4.5% and rolling reserves of 5-10% held for chargeback protection.

E-Wallets and Alternative Methods

Integrate popular e-wallet solutions like Skrill, Neteller, and local payment methods specific to your target markets. In Southeast Asia, bank transfers and local wallets dominate. In Latin America, PIX and local bank integrations are essential. Each market has preferred payment methods, and supporting them directly impacts your conversion rate.

Cryptocurrency Payments

Crypto deposits offer advantages including instant processing, lower fees, and accessibility in regions with limited banking infrastructure. Support major cryptocurrencies (BTC, ETH, USDT) through established crypto payment processors. Implement automatic conversion to fiat to avoid exchange rate exposure, and ensure your AML procedures cover crypto transaction monitoring.

Tags: payment systemsprocessingbank cardscrypto paymentsacquiring
